evals.prompt: Create a Drupal module called event_notifications that sends email notifications when new Event content is published. It needs a service for the notification logic, a hook for node insert, and a config form to set the recipient email address.
evals.expected_output: Generates: (1) event_notifications.info.yml with hooks_converted: true; (2) event_notifications.services.yml with notification service and hook class; (3) src/NotificationService.php using constructor property promotion and readonly, with injected mail_manager and config.factory; (4) src/Hook/EventNotificationsHooks.php with #[Hook('node_insert')] method; (5) src/Form/NotificationSettingsForm.php extending ConfigFormBase; (6) config/schema/event_notifications.schema.yml; (7) event_notifications.routing.yml for the settings form. All PHP uses type declarations, PHP 8.4+ patterns.

evals.prompt: I need a new module called featured_content that provides a Block plugin. The block should show the 5 most recent nodes tagged with a 'Featured' taxonomy term. Use dependency injection, not static calls.
evals.expected_output: Generates: (1) featured_content.info.yml with hooks_converted: true; (2) featured_content.services.yml (if needed); (3) src/Plugin/Block/FeaturedContentBlock.php with #[Block(...)] PHP attribute (not Doctrine annotation), implementing ContainerFactoryPluginInterface, using constructor property promotion, with injected entity_type.manager; (4) render array with proper #cache tags and contexts. No \Drupal:: static calls anywhere.

evals.prompt: Scaffold a module named api_sync that has a Drush command to pull data from an external API and create/update nodes. It needs: a service for the API client, a Drush command, and a queue worker to process items in batches.
evals.expected_output: Generates: (1) api_sync.info.yml; (2) api_sync.services.yml with API client service and hook class; (3) src/ApiClient.php with injected http_client and config.factory, using constructor property promotion; (4) src/Commands/ApiSyncCommands.php with #[AsCommand] or Drush attributes, using AutowireTrait; (5) src/Plugin/QueueWorker/ApiSyncWorker.php with #[QueueWorker(...)] PHP attribute; (6) config/schema if config is introduced. All modern PHP patterns.
